result.note: REAL per-frame ANCHOR-quality render times for frame indices [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7] of the animated 'classroom' shot (1920x1080, nframes=8, draft_spp=512, adaptive_thr=0.02, denoiser=oidn, guides=True, light_tree=True, bounces=12, cam_motion=1.0). Same DOLLY+PAN+YAW camera keyframes and same anchor stack as exp_render_stack.py so frame t is the keystone's frame t. Every per_frame_render_s is a real time.perf_counter() whole-subprocess wall-clock (launch + .blend load + BVH + adaptive trace + in-pipeline denoise + EXR encode, NO exclusions). t_subset_render_s = summed render wall-clock of the assigned frames; t_pod_subprocess_s = the whole per-pod span this runner saw (bootstrap + scene fetch + all frame renders). modeled:false — no reprojection or crop model here; each assigned frame is rendered FULLY and timed.

note: REAL multi-pod DISTRIBUTION of the animated 'classroom' shot (1920x1080, 8 frames, anchor stack: adaptive_thr=0.02, oidn denoiser, guides=True, light_tree=True, draft_spp=512, bounces=12) across 3 REAL RunPod GPUs. Frames split [[0, 1, 2], [3, 4, 5], [6, 7]] (even=False). T_serial_single_pod=142.2s = the same frames rendered back-to-back on ONE pod (measured_on_pod0_this_run). T_real_distributed_including_provisioning=898.4s = first provisioning call to last pod finishing (cold start: provision+reach+ship+deps+serial-baseline+distributed render). T_real_distributed_excluding_provisioning=72.7s = fleet-ready to last pod finishing (steady-state throughput). provision_tax_s=825.7s is reported SEPARATELY. net_speedup_real = T_serial_single_pod / T_real_distributed_excluding_provisioning = 1.96x — the FAIR steady-state comparison, NOT the ideal fan-out ceiling (exp_render_faninout.py reports that upper bound; this is what survives real network + scheduling). Provisioning was sequential. Every per-frame time is a real time.perf_counter() whole-subprocess wall-clock measured ON the pod. CAVEAT: frames do not divide evenly across pods (8 frames / 3 pods -> shares [3, 3, 2]); the pod with 3 frames bounds the distributed wall-clock, so net_speedup_real is capped below N by the uneven split.
